info.name: 010 Permissions Testing - Employee-Only Access
info.description: Uses shared users from 000 (customer, supplier) and 001 (admin). Run 000 and 001 first; no user creation. Run 000 with a Postman environment selected and complete: (1) Supplier Setup (Register Supplier Institution, Register Supplier User, etc.) so supplierUsername, supplierPassword are set; (2) Client Setup (Customer Signup Request, Get Dev Pending Token, Customer Signup Verify) so customerUsername, customerPassword, customerUserId are set; then run 010 with the same environment. Runs last (after 001-009) because it archives the customer at the end. Tests employee-only access (Plans, Credit Currency, Discretionary) by role_type and role_name. Fintech Link has been deprecated; subscription payment uses POST /subscriptions/with-payment instead.

// Find any available token so Setup works across collections that use different names.

item.name: Discretionary - Approve (Employee Vianda Admin) - Should Fail

exec: // Set Authorization header from collection variable
throw new Error("testDiscretionaryRequestId not set. Run 'Discretionary - POST Create Request' first.");
// Expected: 403 (role check — Vianda Admin lacks super-admin permission for approve).
// On re-runs the request may already be approved from a prior pass, yielding 400
// "Cannot approve request with status: approved". That also proves no actual state change;
// accept both codes as "approve was blocked".
pm.test("Approve blocked for non-super-admin (403) or request already terminal (400 re-run)", function () {
// Use shared users from 000 and 001 (env or globals) - no new user creation
const adminTok = pm.environment.get('adminAuthToken') || pm.globals.get('adminAuthToken') || pm.environment.get('authToken') || pm.globals.get('authToken');
if (!cu || String(cu).trim() === '') { cu = 'customer'; pm.environment.set('customerUsername', cu); pm.globals.set('customerUsername', cu); }
if (!cp || String(cp).trim() === '') { cp = 'Customer123!'; pm.environment.set('customerPassword', cp); pm.globals.set('customerPassword', cp); }
const custTok = pm.environment.get('customerAuthToken') || pm.globals.get('customerAuthToken') || pm.environment.get('clientUserAuthToken') || pm.globals.get('clientUserAuthToken');
// Only set institutionBUserId from customerUserId when not already set (Scope Comparison's GET /users/ sets it from response — prefer that, it is guaranteed to exist)
